title: elymus trachycaulus subsp. virescens
intro: Elymus trachycaulus subsp. virescens, also known as green wildrye, is a perennial grass species native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and seeds of the green wildrye can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
